summary.goal: To create a CLI tool named llmgraph that organizes LLM-assisted development work into a versioned, navigable DAG of conversation nodes tied to a software project.
decisions: One JSON file per node under .llmgraph/nodes/<uuid>.json for human-readable and git-diffable history.
graph.json holds the DAG edges and root/active node pointers.
state.json (untracked) holds the active node for the current session.
All file I/O goes through a GraphStore abstraction to allow swapping storage backends later, with SQLite as the obvious v2 target.
Git integration uses subprocess calls to `git` rather than libgit2 to keep the dependency footprint small.
The context payload command (llmgraph context) walks the ancestor path from root to target node and formats it for pasting directly into a new LLM conversation.
